rationale: We use 20 identity-centered, MECE-leaning categories that mirror how working researchers and clinicians conventionally carve the landscape: core disciplines, engineering domains, the health continuum, environment/earth/ecology, computation/methods, and social/behavioral/policy fields. Each bucket is large enough to matter but not so broad that a single card dominates new-paper volume. We deliberately merged closely entwined areas (e.g., chemistry with materials; neuroscience with cognitive psychology) and kept engineering in three practice-oriented blocks to reduce overlap with science. We did not split medicine by specialty, physics from astronomy, or carve out cross-cutting themes like “energy” at level 1; those live at level 2.

categories.name: Mathematics
categories.tagline: Pure, applied, and computational math across fields.
covers: All branches of pure/applied math and mathematical modeling.
Examples: Annals of Mathematics, SIAM Journal, arXiv:math.
explicitly_excludes: Statistics methods — Data Science, Statistics & Methods
Theoretical CS proofs — Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence
Mathematical physics primarily about phenomena — Physics & Astronomy

categories.name: Physics & Astronomy
categories.tagline: Matter, energy, and the universe from quarks to cosmos.
covers: Experimental, theoretical, and computational physics; astronomy and astrophysics.
Examples: Physical Review Letters, ApJ, arXiv:hep-th/astro-ph.
explicitly_excludes: Materials properties emphasis — Chemistry & Materials
Planetary geology — Earth & Planetary Sciences
Medical imaging trials — Clinical Medicine & Surgery

categories.name: Chemistry & Materials
categories.tagline: Molecules, reactions, and structure–property materials science.
covers: Synthesis, catalysis, analytical, physical chemistry; materials design and characterization.
Examples: JACS, Angewandte, Advanced Materials, ACS Nano.
explicitly_excludes: Process scale-up and reactors — Chemical & Bioengineering
Biomolecular mechanisms in organisms — Biological Sciences
Clinical pharmacology outcomes — Clinical Medicine & Surgery

categories.name: Earth & Planetary Sciences
categories.tagline: Solid Earth, oceans, atmosphere, and planetary systems.
covers: Geology, geophysics, geochemistry, oceanography, atmospheric and planetary science.
Examples: JGR, Geology, Nature Geoscience, Icarus.
explicitly_excludes: Ecological dynamics and biodiversity — Ecology & Evolution
Environmental policy or LCA — Environmental Science & Sustainability
Astrophysics and cosmology — Physics & Astronomy
Paleobiology focused on evolution — Ecology & Evolution

categories.name: Environmental Science & Sustainability
categories.tagline: Climate, pollution, resources, and sustainability solutions.
covers: Climate impacts, pollution and toxicology, conservation policy, LCA and sustainability science.
Examples: Environmental Science & Technology, Nature Sustainability.
explicitly_excludes: Ecosystem dynamics and evolution — Ecology & Evolution
Civil/environmental infrastructure design — Mechanical, Civil & Aerospace Engineering
Population health exposures — Public Health & Epidemiology
Fundamental geoscience — Earth & Planetary Sciences

categories.name: Ecology & Evolution
categories.tagline: Organisms, ecosystems, biodiversity, and evolutionary change.
covers: Population/community ecology, macroecology, phylogenetics, conservation biology, paleobiology.
Examples: Ecology Letters, Evolution, Systematic Biology.
explicitly_excludes: Agronomy and crop systems — Agriculture, Food & Nutrition
Human clinical microbiome outcomes — Clinical Medicine & Surgery
Environmental policy assessment — Environmental Science & Sustainability
Molecular mechanisms in model organisms — Biological Sciences

categories.name: Biological Sciences
categories.tagline: Molecular, cellular, genetic, and organismal life science.
covers: Molecular/cellular biology, genetics, genomics, biochemistry, microbiology, systems and developmental biology.
Examples: Cell, Nature Genetics, PNAS (biological sciences).
explicitly_excludes: Clinical diagnosis, treatment, trials — Clinical Medicine & Surgery
Population health and policy — Public Health & Epidemiology
Neural systems and cognition — Brain & Cognitive Sciences
Bioprocess and biomanufacturing — Chemical & Bioengineering
Ecology and evolution — Ecology & Evolution

categories.name: Brain & Cognitive Sciences
categories.tagline: Neuroscience, cognition, behavior, and language science.
covers: Cellular to systems neuroscience, cognitive psychology, psycholinguistics, computational neuroscience.
Examples: Neuron, Nature Neuroscience, Psychological Science.
explicitly_excludes: Neurology/psychiatry clinical studies — Clinical Medicine & Surgery
Population mental health — Public Health & Epidemiology
AI method development — Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence
Molecular biology outside nervous system — Biological Sciences

categories.name: Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence
categories.tagline: Algorithms, systems, ML/AI, and human–computer interaction.
covers: Theory, systems, networks, security, databases, graphics, ML/AI, HCI, NLP, robotics.
Examples: NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, SIGCOMM, arXiv:cs.
explicitly_excludes: General statistics methodology — Data Science, Statistics & Methods
Hardware circuits and devices — Electrical & Electronic Engineering
Applied domain studies using standard models — Send to that domain
Clinical AI trials in patients — Clinical Medicine & Surgery

categories.name: Data Science, Statistics & Methods
categories.tagline: Statistical theory, data methods, and scientific software.
covers: Statistical methodology, causal inference, data mining, benchmarking, open-source tools.
Examples: JASA, Biometrika, Journal of Statistical Software.
explicitly_excludes: Core CS algorithmics/ML — Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence
Domain-driven empirical analyses — Send to that domain
Biostatistical analyses of trials — Clinical Medicine & Surgery

categories.name: Electrical & Electronic Engineering
categories.tagline: Circuits, devices, communications, signals, and power systems.
covers: Semiconductors, photonics, RF, control, signal processing, communications, power and smart grids.
Examples: IEEE T-MTT, T-Comm, Nature Electronics.
explicitly_excludes: Computer systems/software focus — Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence
Materials synthesis and chemistry — Chemistry & Materials
Policy/regulatory energy transitions — Environmental Science & Sustainability

categories.name: Mechanical, Civil & Aerospace Engineering
categories.tagline: Structures, fluids, transport, design, and infrastructure.
covers: Solid/structural mechanics, fluid dynamics, thermal sciences, robotics design, transportation, infrastructure.
Examples: Journal of Fluid Mechanics, ASCE journals, AIAA journals.
explicitly_excludes: Materials discovery and chemistry — Chemistry & Materials
Environmental impact science — Environmental Science & Sustainability
Process/bioprocess engineering — Chemical & Bioengineering

categories.name: Chemical & Bioengineering
categories.tagline: Reactions, separations, bioprocesses, and pharmaceutical engineering.
covers: Process design, reaction engineering, separations, metabolic and tissue engineering, scale-up.
Examples: AIChE Journal, Biotechnology & Bioengineering.
explicitly_excludes: Molecular chemistry and catalysis — Chemistry & Materials
Clinical device/drug trials — Clinical Medicine & Surgery
Environmental policy or LCA — Environmental Science & Sustainability

categories.name: Clinical Medicine & Surgery
categories.tagline: Diagnosis, treatment, trials, and patient-centered outcomes.
covers: All clinical specialties, trials, guidelines, imaging, surgery, precision and translational medicine in patients.
Examples: NEJM, The Lancet, JAMA, Cochrane Reviews.
explicitly_excludes: Population-level health and systems — Public Health & Epidemiology
Basic mechanistic biology — Biological Sciences
Biomedical device engineering studies — Chemical & Bioengineering
Cognitive/behavioral lab studies — Brain & Cognitive Sciences

categories.name: Public Health & Epidemiology
categories.tagline: Population health, epidemiology, policy, and health services.
covers: Epidemiology, health services research, implementation science, global health, health policy and systems.
Examples: The Lancet Public Health, AJPH, BMJ Global Health.
explicitly_excludes: Clinical interventions in patients — Clinical Medicine & Surgery
Health economics methodology — Economics & Finance
Environmental science mechanistic fate/transport — Environmental Science & Sustainability

categories.name: Economics & Finance
categories.tagline: Economic theory, econometrics, markets, and financial systems.
covers: Micro/macro, econometrics, development, labor, finance, behavioral and experimental economics.
Examples: QJE, Econometrica, Journal of Finance, NBER papers.
explicitly_excludes: Management and strategy — Business & Management
Public administration and political institutions — Society, Politics & Policy
Population health policy focus — Public Health & Epidemiology

categories.name: Society, Politics & Policy
categories.tagline: Sociology, anthropology, politics, law, and public policy.
covers: Sociology, anthropology, archaeology, demography, political science, law, criminology, public administration.
Examples: American Sociological Review, World Politics, Journal of Law & Economics.
explicitly_excludes: Economics and finance — Economics & Finance
Education science — Education Research
Population health systems — Public Health & Epidemiology
Business/organizational studies — Business & Management

categories.name: Business & Management
categories.tagline: Organizations, strategy, operations, marketing, and accounting.
covers: Management science, organizational behavior, operations, supply chains, marketing, accounting, IS in firms.
Examples: Management Science, Strategic Management Journal, JOM.
explicitly_excludes: Core econometrics/economic theory — Economics & Finance
Algorithmic methods research — Data Science, Statistics & Methods
HCI and systems software — Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence

categories.name: Education Research
categories.tagline: Learning science, pedagogy, assessment, and education policy.
covers: K–12 and higher-ed research, curricula, assessment, learning analytics, education policy.
Examples: Review of Educational Research, Learning & Instruction.
explicitly_excludes: Cognitive mechanisms of learning — Brain & Cognitive Sciences
Economics of education methods — Economics & Finance
EdTech algorithm development — Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence

categories.name: Agriculture, Food & Nutrition
categories.tagline: Crops, livestock, food systems, and nutrition science.
covers: Agronomy, animal science, food chemistry/engineering, postharvest, nutrition and diet science.
Examples: Field Crops Research, Animal, AJCN, Food Chemistry.
explicitly_excludes: Clinical nutrition in patients — Clinical Medicine & Surgery
Population nutrition policy — Public Health & Epidemiology
Basic molecular biology — Biological Sciences
Environmental impact assessments — Environmental Science & Sustainability

uncovered_concerns: Astronomy separated from physics was considered; kept unified to avoid splitting arXiv subfields and journals that span both.
Neuroscience vs. psychology split was considered; merged into Brain & Cognitive Sciences to minimize boundary disputes with clinical neurology/psychiatry.
Materials Science as its own top-level was considered; merged with Chemistry to reduce chemistry–materials overlap.
Energy as a top-level theme was considered; dispersed across Environmental Science (systems/policy), ECE (grids/power electronics), and Chemical/Mechanical Engineering (devices/process).
Bioinformatics/computational biology as a top-level was considered; method papers go to CS/AI or Data & Statistics, question-driven biology to Biological Sciences.

Pharmacology/toxicology as a separate top-level was considered; preclinical mechanisms go to Biological Sciences, environmental tox to Environmental Science & Sustainability, clinical pharmacology to Clinical Medicine.
Nursing, dentistry, veterinary medicine were not split at level 1; routed under Clinical Medicine & Surgery to avoid over-fragmentation.
Geography split (physical vs. human) was not created; physical geography maps to Earth & Planetary or Environmental Science, human geography to Society, Politics & Policy.
Linguistics was not a standalone card; experimental/psycholinguistics sits in Brain & Cognitive Sciences, sociolinguistics in Society, Politics & Policy.
Information/Library Science and Scientometrics were not split; routed to Society, Politics & Policy or Data Science & Methods depending on emphasis.
